title: matricaria inodora var. agrestis
intro: Matricaria inodora var. agrestis, also known as wild chamomile, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae, characterized by its small white flowers and apple-like fragrance. The medicinal properties of Matricaria inodora var. agrestis lie in its essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing effects. The plant is used to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate menstrual cramps, skin irritations, and digestive issues. In herbal preparations, Matricaria inodora var. agrestis is often used as a tea, tincture, or topical cream to harness its health benefits.
